groupVariants.label: Affluent Professionals
groupVariants.summary: High-income urban and suburban professionals aged 40–58, career-established, leaning female. Value quality, authenticity, and premium experiences.

research.insights: Affluent 45–60 consumers respond strongly to authenticity and craft narratives. For food/beverage brands, provenance and quality cues outperform price messaging. Warm colour palettes and slower pacing signal premium positioning. This segment over-indexes on Connected TV and premium streaming — ad recall is 34% higher when the creative matches the platform's editorial tone.

changes.summary: Warm cinematic grade — golden tones reinforce premium feel
changes.reason: Affluent segment responds to warm, film-like colour for premium brands

changes.summary: Slight slowdown (0.88×) for deliberate, confident pacing
changes.reason: Older high-income viewers prefer measured pacing over fast cuts
Quality/craft message resonates with this segment per research

groupVariants.label: Young Urban Creatives
groupVariants.summary: Single renters aged 19–29 in major metro areas — students, early-career tech and creative workers. Mobile-first, trend-aware, value-driven.

research.insights: Gen Z and young Millennials engage most with fast-cut, high-energy content. Organic, lo-fi aesthetics outperform polished production in this segment — perceived authenticity is the primary trust signal. Text overlays with punchy, irreverent copy drive 2.1× higher share rate on short-form platforms. This audience skips ads after 3 seconds; front-load the brand moment.

changes.summary: High-contrast cool grade — punchy, social-native look
changes.reason: Cool high-contrast palette matches Gen Z aesthetic preferences
Fast cuts maintain attention for short-form mobile viewing
Irreverent copy resonates with Gen Z brand voice expectations

groupVariants.label: Suburban Family Segment
groupVariants.summary: Mid-income married parents aged 30–45 in suburban areas — homeowners with kids, practical and value-conscious but open to aspirational moments.

research.insights: Suburban parents aged 30–45 are the heaviest CPG purchasers by household volume. Family togetherness and everyday ritual are the strongest emotional hooks — ads that show real family moments outperform aspirational lifestyle by 28% in purchase intent. Moderate pacing with clear product visibility and a functional headline drives highest conversion. This segment watches more Connected TV than any other.

changes.summary: Natural warm grade — approachable, everyday feel
changes.reason: Warm natural tones signal authenticity and family comfort
Family togetherness is the top purchase driver for this segment
Early brand visibility reduces skip rate on CTV placements
